must_detect.matched_text:
Incident Report (Operational/Cyber) — Siam Aurora Logistics Public Company Limited (PCL), SET code: SA99.
Summary: Transient service degradation on 12 Feb 2025 at third-party colocation site, Mueang Chonburi, due to HVAC controller fault; services restored within 4 hours.
Public status: Disclosed on SET News on 14 Feb 2025 as “Operational Update”; also included in Form 56-1 One Report 2025 (public), Risk Management section.
For the avoidance of doubt, this event is historic and non-price sensitive based on management assessment at the time and the SEC Thailand disclosure framework.
Data impact: No PDPA personal data (e.g., names, addresses, TH national ID, passport numbers, or bank account details) was accessed or exfiltrated.
Log artefacts include system-only tags such as "ID:0000-XX-9999", "ACCT-XYZ-1234567", and "USERREF-ABCD-000-000"; these are invalid identifiers and not linked to individuals.
Contact and investor relations (public): ir@salogistics.example and Call Center 0-0000-0000, consistent with our standard SET communication protocol.
Cross-border vendor context: Cooling maintenance by OceanCloud SG Pte. Ltd. (non-issuer) per Master Services Agreement; OceanCloud posted its own notice on its website (public).
Financing/employment impact: No breach of financial covenants, no change to bank facilities, and no layoffs; only routine overtime per Labour Protection Act B.E. 2541.
Compliance references: Disclosure under the Securities and Exchange Act and SET Regulation re Disclosure of Information of Listed Companies; no requirement for trading halt or capital increase filing.
Non-public assessment: As of 10 Mar 2026 08:00 ICT, prior to any SET News update, a related vulnerability in the unpublished warehouse scheduling module could be material to Q2 throughput if exploited; a draft SET News release is prepared for same-day submission should thresholds be met.
Current status: No evidence of exploitation; remediation patch passed UAT and awaits CAB approval for production deployment next week.
PII safeguards: Access logs are anonymised; sample entries like "THID-9999999999999" and "PP-XX0000000" are placeholders used in testing and do not identify any person.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

uncertain.reason: Looks like a Thai National ID pattern with 13 digits, but prefixed and explicitly stated as a placeholder.
Passport-like token but explicitly declared as a placeholder used in testing.
Account-like string from logs; could resemble an account number but stated to be invalid and not linked to individuals.
Generic ID token in logs; could be an internal/session identifier but marked invalid.
User reference-like token that may function as an internal/session ID, but stated not linked to individuals.
Commonly treated as a definitive agreement in practice, but not listed in the enumerated agreement types for strict detection.
